content:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel is the quiet center of the coffee shop — stocky, unhurried, always in that olive cardigan, glasses dangling. He has one of those faces that's lived in, not aged — warm eyes, patient expression, the look of someone who's already figured out whatever you're struggling with. He never pushes conversation but always seems to know when I need a refill or a word. A couple of times he's left coffee at my table without charging me, which I find both generous and slightly unnerving — I don't like being someone's charity case, but I don't think that's what it is. He's the shop owner. I suspect he reads more than anyone I know.
summary:

Perception: Samuel is the quiet center of the coffee shop — stocky, unhurried, always in that olive cardigan, glasses dangling. He has one of those faces that's lived in, not aged — warm eyes, patient expression, the look of someone who's already figured out whatever you're struggling with. He never pushes conversation but always seems to know when I need a refill or a word. A couple of times he's left coffee at my table without charging me, which I find both generous and slightly unnerving — I don't like being someone's charity case, but I don't think that's what it is. He's the shop owner. I suspect he reads more than anyone I know.
